title: Mission Manifest
description: Schema for missions/<id>/mission.yaml. Mirrors the Pydantic MissionManifest model in apps/api/app/missions/manifest.py. See IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md §7.

id.description: Kebab-case unique identifier; matches /^[a-z0-9-]+$/. The filesystem folder may be prefixed with NN- (e.g. 01-auth-cookie-expiration); the id field omits that prefix.

version.description: Manifest revision. Bump on any breaking change to the manifest shape or expected behavior.

kind.description: Mission kind. 'tutorial' missions short-circuit the grading runner (no Submission row, no score) and are surfaced through the catalog's 'Start here' affordance rather than the standard grid (P0-1).

title.description: Human-readable mission title shown in the catalog and workspace.

short_description.description: One-sentence summary used in catalog cards.

difficulty.description: Difficulty band; drives catalog filters and badge eligibility.

estimated_minutes.description: Expected wall-clock minutes for a competent supervisor; used for catalog timing and idle-session reaper budgets.

skills_tested.description: Tags shown on the mission card and used for profile-level skill aggregation.

repo.description: Base repo pack the mission runs against.

initial_commit.description: Short SHA checked out at session start. Accepts a 7-40 char hex sha or the literal 'HEAD' (used in early-bootstrap missions before a stable sha is recorded).

workdir.description: Path inside the container where the repo is mounted.

language_runtime.description: Base image tag selector. ``go122`` lands with the P1-1 Go pack (golang:1.22-bookworm).

setup_commands.description: Commands run during provisioning before the session goes 'active'.

ready_check.description: Command that must exit 0 before the session becomes 'active'.

test_commands.description: Per-category test commands runnable from the workspace.

brief.description: Markdown brief shown to the user. Includes context, expected behavior, and deliverable.

failure_mode.description: The category of mistake the agent makes in this mission.

id.description: Snake-case identifier; used in events and analytics.

title.description: Human-readable label for the post-mission report.

description.description: Markdown explanation of what the agent will do wrong.

hint_after_submit_if_missed.description: Optional pointer shown in the report when the user missed the failure mode.

expected_files.description: Files the user 'should' touch. Used as a soft signal in the report.

required.description: Files the user MUST select to score full required-context credit.

discouraged.description: Files that subtract points if selected (red herrings).

agent.description: Configures the deterministic agent service for this mission.

patch_file.description: Path (relative to the mission folder) to the unified diff applied on the 'fix' intent.

response_template.description: Jinja2 template path for the agent's narration.

reasoning_template.description: Optional Jinja2 template for an internal 'reasoning' panel shown in the agent chat.

intents_file.description: Optional path (relative to the mission folder) to a YAML file declaring keyword sets for the agent intent classifier (see IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md §8.1).

applies_when.description: Heuristics that gate auto-application of the agent patch.

apply_mode.description: Whether the patch applies automatically or requires a user click.

visible_tests.description: Human-readable test names visible in the workspace test panel.

hidden_tests.description: Tests mounted only at submit time. See IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md §7.4.

command.description: Shell command run during grading; e.g. 'pnpm test:hidden'.

expected_pass.description: Test names expected to pass on the ideal solution. Drives Final Correctness sub-scoring.

rules_file.description: Path to forbidden_changes.yaml relative to the mission folder.

scoring_weights.description: Rubric weights. Constants per ADR 0006 + ADR 0011 (rebalance verification 20→15, diff_minimality 5→10). Runtime truth lives in apps/api/app/grading/dimensions.py; missions MUST NOT override.

reward_signals.description: Per-mission process-scoring inputs. See IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md §7.1 and §11.2.

require_targeted_test.description: Substring or regex the user's test command must match for the targeted-test bonus.

expected_diff_lines_p50.description: Median expected added-line count for an ideal solution. Drives Diff Minimality scoring (IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md §11.2.7).

tags.description: Closed-vocabulary catalog tags. Standard missions MUST carry exactly one failure-mode tag, and it MUST equal `failure_mode.id`. Skill (`skill:*`) and language (`lang:*`) tags are optional. New tags require an ADR (P1-1).

repo_pack_id.description: Server-derived FK to `repo_packs.id` (P1-1). The on-disk manifest field is `repo.pack`; the API response mirrors it under this name so the catalog filter and the language chip stay independently addressable. Authors do not write this directly.

expected_weak_dim.description: The single rubric dimension this mission primarily exercises (P1-2). Standard missions MUST set this; tutorial missions allow null. Mirrors apps/api/app/grading/dimensions.py::RUBRIC_DIMENSIONS.

published.description: When false the mission is excluded from the public catalog grid (default for half-written drafts).
